title: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection') vulnerability in Profelis Information...
description:
Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection') vulnerability in Profelis Information and Consulting Trade and Industry Limited Company SambaBox allows OS Command Injection.

This issue affects SambaBox: from 5.1 before 5.3.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary OS commands on the affected SambaBox versions, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, system compromise, or disruption of services.
recommended_actions: Upgrade SambaBox to version 5.3 or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Implement strict input validation and sanitization to prevent code injection attacks.
Monitor system logs and network traffic for signs of exploitation or unusual activity related to this vulnerability.

title: A vulnerability was detected in Totolink N300RH 3.2.4-B20220812. This vulnerability affects the...
description: A vulnerability was detected in Totolink N300RH 3.2.4-B20220812. This vulnerability affects the function setMacFilterRules of the file /cgi-bin/cstecgi.cgi of the component POST Request Handler. The manipulation of the argument mac_address results in buffer overflow. The attack may be launched remotely. The exploit is now public and may be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Totolink N300RH 3.2.4-B20220812 allows remote attackers to exploit a buffer overflow via the mac_address parameter in the setMacFilterRules function. This could lead to arbitrary code execution, denial of service, or unauthorized access to the device, potentially compromising the network it is connected to.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply any available firmware updates or patches provided by Totolink to address CVE-2026-7750.
Implement network segmentation to isolate vulnerable devices from critical infrastructure and sensitive systems.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity and consider deploying intrusion detection/prevention systems to detect potential exploitation attempts.

title: A heap buffer overflow vulnerability exists in the DTLS handshake fragment reassembly logic of...
description: A heap buffer overflow vulnerability exists in the DTLS handshake fragment reassembly logic of GnuTLS. The issue arises in merge_handshake_packet() where incoming handshake fragments are matched and merged based solely on handshake type, without validating that the message_length field remains consistent across all fragments of the same logical message. An attacker can exploit this by sending crafted DTLS fragments with conflicting message_length values, causing the implementation to allocate a

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The heap buffer overflow vulnerability in the DTLS handshake fragment reassembly logic of GnuTLS could allow attackers to execute arbitrary code, cause a denial of service, or compromise the confidentiality and integrity of secure communications. This poses a significant risk to systems relying on GnuTLS for secure data transmission.
recommended_actions: Immediately update to the latest patched version of GnuTLS that addresses CVE-2026-33846.
Implement network-level protections, such as intrusion detection and prevention systems (IDS/IPS), to monitor and block malicious DTLS traffic.
Conduct a security review of all systems and applications using GnuTLS to ensure they are not exposed to this vulnerability.

title: A security flaw has been discovered in Totolink N300RH 3.2.4-B20220812. Affected by this...
description: A security flaw has been discovered in Totolink N300RH 3.2.4-B20220812. Affected by this vulnerability is the function loginauth of the file /cgi-bin/cstecgi.cgi of the component Parameter Handler. Performing a manipulation of the argument Password results in buffer overflow. The attack can be initiated remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via buffer overflow, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data theft, or complete system compromise.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the Totolink N300RH firmware to the latest version if a patch is available.
Implement network segmentation to isolate vulnerable devices and limit exposure to external threats.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity and apply intrusion detection/prevention systems to mitigate exploitation attempts.

title: A vulnerability was found in CodeAstro Online Classroom 1.0. This affects an unknown function of...
description: A vulnerability was found in CodeAstro Online Classroom 1.0. This affects an unknown function of the file /OnlineClassroom/addnewstudent. The manipulation of the argument fname results in sql injection. The attack may be performed from remote. The exploit has been made public and could be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote attackers to perform SQL injection via the 'fname' parameter in the 'addnewstudent' function, potentially leading to unauthorized access to sensitive data or database manipulation.
recommended_actions: Immediately update CodeAstro Online Classroom to the latest patched version if available.
Implement input validation and sanitization to prevent SQL injection vulnerabilities.
Monitor logs for suspicious activities and consider deploying a web application firewall (WAF) to block malicious requests.

title: A flaw has been found in CodeAstro Online Classroom 1.0. The affected element is an unknown...

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows for SQL injection through the 'fid' parameter in the faculty login functionality of CodeAstro Online Classroom 1.0. If exploited, attackers could gain unauthorized access to sensitive data, modify database contents, or disrupt application functionality.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply input validation and parameterized queries to sanitize the 'fid' parameter and prevent SQL injection.
Update to a patched version of CodeAstro Online Classroom if available, or contact the vendor for a security fix.
Monitor application logs for unusual activity, such as unexpected database queries, to detect potential exploitation attempts.

title: A weakness has been identified in justdan96 tsMuxer up to 2.7.0. This vulnerability affects the...
description: A weakness has been identified in justdan96 tsMuxer up to 2.7.0. This vulnerability affects the function HevcVpsUnit::setFPS of the file /AFLplusplus/tsMuxer_prev/tsMuxer/hevc.cpp. This manipulation of the argument track_id causes denial of service. The attack requires local access. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. This vulnerability only affects products that are no longer supported by the maintainer.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in justdan96 tsMuxer up to version 2.7.0 could lead to a denial of service if exploited locally. However, the exploit requires local access and affects unsupported software, limiting its overall impact.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to alternative supported software or newer versions of tsMuxer if available.
Restrict local access to systems running outdated versions of tsMuxer.
Monitor systems for unusual activity and ensure proper access controls are in place.

title: A security vulnerability has been detected in justdan96 tsMuxer up to 2.7.0. This issue affects...
description: A security vulnerability has been detected in justdan96 tsMuxer up to 2.7.0. This issue affects the function VvcVpsUnit::setFPS of the file tsMuxer/vvc.cpp. Such manipulation of the argument track_id leads to denial of service. An attack has to be approached locally. However, the exploit requires local access and affects software that is no longer supported by the maintainer, reducing its overall impact and likelihood of widespread exploitation.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to alternative or actively maintained software solutions to replace justdan96 tsMuxer.
Restrict local access to systems running vulnerable versions of tsMuxer to trusted personnel only.
Monitor for any further updates or patches from the community or third-party maintainers.

title: A critical IDOR vulnerability has been discovered in Comet Backup affecting all versions from 20...
description: A critical IDOR vulnerability has been discovered in Comet Backup affecting all versions from 20.11.0 to 26.1.1 and 26.2.1. The vulnerability allows a tenant administrator to impersonate any end-user account of other tenants on the same server via a vulnerable API call.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The IDOR vulnerability in Comet Backup allows tenant administrators to impersonate end-user accounts of other tenants, potentially leading to unauthorized data access, data breaches, and privilege escalation. This could compromise sensitive information and undermine trust in multi-tenant environments.
recommended_actions: Immediately update Comet Backup to the latest patched version once available or apply any official mitigation steps provided by the vendor.
Restrict API access to trusted IPs and monitor API logs for unusual activity, particularly impersonation attempts.
Perform a security audit of tenant configurations and permissions to identify and mitigate any potential misuse.

title: In mutt before 2.3.2, the imap_auth_gss security level is mishandled.
description: In mutt before 2.3.2, the imap_auth_gss security level is mishandled.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The mishandling of the imap_auth_gss security level in mutt before version 2.3.2 could potentially allow improper authentication handling, leading to a minor security vulnerability. However, the severity is low as exploitation scenarios are limited and require specific configurations.
recommended_actions: Upgrade mutt to version 2.3.2 or later to address the vulnerability.
Review and audit email client configurations to ensure secure authentication settings are in place.
Monitor for any unusual authentication activity in email systems that might indicate exploitation attempts.

title: CVE-2026-42363 - An insufficient encryption vulnerability exists in the Device Authentication functionality of GeoVis
description:
An insufficient encryption vulnerability exists in the Device Authentication functionality of GeoVision GV-IP Device Utility 9.0.5. Listening to broadcast packets can lead to credentials leak. An attacker can listen to broadcast messages to trigger this vulnerability.


When interacting with various Geovision devices on the network, the utility may send privileged commands; in order to do so, the username and password of the device need to be provided. In some instances the command is broadcasted over UDP and the username/password are encrypted using a cryptographic protocol that appears to be derivated from Blowfish. However the symmetric key used for the encryption is also included in the packet, and thus the security of the username/password only relies on the "obscurity" of the encryption scheme. An attacker on the same LAN can listen to the broadcast traffic once an admin user interacts with the device, and decrypt the credentials using their own implementation of the algorithm. With this password the attacker would have full control over the device configuration, allowing them to change its ip address or even reset it to factory default.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-42363.
